steps.title: Separate the two clauses
steps.content: The sentence first says the idea did not originate with Collier. It then gives the actual origin: in 1985, the idea was first articulated in Levy's book. These are independent clauses that need strong separation.

steps.title: Place though as an aside
steps.content: Choice A makes "though" introduce the following time clause instead of ending the first idea. Choice C leaves two independent clauses joined only by commas. Choice D puts the semicolon before "though," creating a dependent clause after it. The semicolon should follow parenthetical "though." The answer is B.
